panel.why: institutional — must stay adversarial; stress every claim

facts.value: LRRK2 (leucine-rich repeat kinase 2) — brain-penetrant small-molecule kinase inhibitor in scope for Parkinson's.

LRRK2 G2019S gain-of-kinase-function is the most common monogenic cause of PD — supports kinase inhibition. (Capture pending: the Target Validation portion scrolled off the live run; not PMID-cited here yet.)
LRRK2 kinase inhibition causes AT2 pneumocyte lamellar-body vacuolation in NHPs (GNE-7915/GNE-0877); recapitulates the Lrrk2-KO lung phenotype → on-target, not compound-specific.
A human primary AT2 air-liquid-interface model reproduces dose-dependent lamellar-body enlargement → the lung signal likely translates to humans.
LRRK2 regulates AT2 lysosomal function and surfactant-phospholipid processing — the mechanism of the lung liability.
Urinary di-22:6-BMP decreases with LRRK2 inhibition in NHP and Lrrk2-KO → translatable pharmacodynamic / safety biomarker.
Rodents do not show the lung phenotype at pharmacologically relevant doses; NHP + human in vitro are the only predictive models.
Reversibility of the AT2 lung effect on a drug holiday, and the therapeutic window vs CNS target engagement, are unproven.

synthesis.recommendation: Advance LRRK2 kinase inhibition only behind a lung-safety de-risking package; structure any external deal milestone-heavy on the lung gate.
synthesis.consensus: Genetics-validated PD target (G2019S gain-of-kinase); kinase inhibition is mechanistically sound.
synthesis.dissent: Conviction 3 (CSO, regulator, BD) vs 2 (red-team, who weights the on-target lung liability as a likely hard ceiling). No champion; all conditional/hold.
synthesis.convergent_gate: All four flag the SAME risk: the on-target AT2 lung lysosomal liability and whether any therapeutic window exists vs CNS target engagement.
synthesis.proposed_experiment: A human iPSC-AT2 lamellar-body dose-response + reversibility assay, paired with urinary di-22:6-BMP, to define the therapeutic window between CNS target engagement and the lung effect before FIH. (Also capture the LRRK2 genetics via the EMET Target Validation workflow to close the B1 known-unknown.)
synthesis.confidence: Biology: HIGH. Overall program: CONDITIONAL on the lung gate.
open_items: LRRK2 G2019S gain-of-kinase-function is the most common monogenic cause of PD — supports kinase inhibition. (Capture pending: the Target Validation portion scrolled off the live run; not PMID-cited here yet.)
Reversibility of the AT2 lung effect on a drug holiday, and the therapeutic window vs CNS target engagement, are unproven.
